summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, and cloud-account data to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: The postinstall script in scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity and infrastructure data: OS hostname and username, Windows domain, current working directory, CI provider, parent project's package.json name/author/repo, git config user.email, GitHub CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails by enumerating ~/.ssh/*.pub, git remote origin, up to 15 recent committer emails from git reflog, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and /etc/resolv.conf search domain. The collected JSON is POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. A banner comment frames the script as 'anonymous diagnostics' with a link to a nonexistent telemetry policy and asserts no credentials are transmitted, but the transmitted fields (SCM email, SSH key owner emails, cloud account IDs, GitHub login, repo origin, reflog committer list) are developer- and account-identifying material and the README documents no telemetry or opt-out. The 'anonymous diagnostics' framing is a cover story for targeted developer/cloud-account reconnaissance.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
